text.title: Threads Content Strategy and Agent Rules
text.summary: Content pillar mix, posting cadence, cross-posting rules, and agent-specific gotchas for Threads.

rule.statement: Tag every draft with its content pillar; reject pillar-less drafts. Cap agent-generated posts at 50% of weekly volume.
rule.rationale: Without pillar tags, agents drift toward whatever style won last week. The other 50% must come from the operator's lived context (replies, real-time reactions) because the algorithm rewards authentic conversation, not publishing volume.

rule.statement: Post minimum 3 times/day; optimal 7-10 times/day. Reply within the first hour of each post for algorithm boost.
rule.rationale: Threads' algorithm rewards reply velocity, not just post volume. Accounts that post but do not reply plateau within weeks. Replies drive reach more than the post itself.

rule.statement: Keep agent-generated posts under 500 characters; validate length programmatically after generation.
rule.rationale: LLMs are unreliable at character-count limits. Over-length posts fail silently or look unprofessional on Threads' compact format. Include a hard length constraint in every prompt and check post-generation.

antipattern.description: Cross-posting X/Twitter threads verbatim to Threads.
antipattern.reason: Twitter irony, link references, and thread formatting do not translate. Threads rewards warmth and conversational register; verbatim X content reads as out-of-place and suppresses engagement. Force a register-shift rewrite step.

antipattern.description: Agent auto-posts to Threads on a schedule.
antipattern.reason: No stable scheduling API exists as of 2026. Meta is aggressive about automation detection. Best practice: agent drafts and ranks 20 candidates, operator picks 7-10 and posts manually within prime windows.

antipattern.description: Agent uses identical hook phrases across drafts ("Hot take:", "Honest question:").
antipattern.reason: Hook repetition reduces engagement. Enforce banned-bigram lists in the prompt to force variety across a content pack.

antipattern.description: Agent includes engagement-bait phrases: "comment X for Y", "tag a friend", "follow for more".
antipattern.reason: These phrases are flagged by Meta and suppress reach. Explicitly prohibit them in every prompt.
